name: Apple Strudel
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Lightly grease a jelly-roll pan.
3. In a large bowl, mix sugar, walnuts, raisins, lemon peel, and cinnamon.
4. Peel, quarter, and core apples; cut into 1/2-inch chunks.
5. Toss apple chunks with the sugar mixture.
6. Place one phyllo sheet on a towel-covered work surface (keep remaining phyllo covered with plastic wrap).
7. Brush entire surface with about 1 tablespoon butter, then sprinkle with 1 tablespoon cracker crumbs.
8. Layer 5 more sheets in the same manner, brushing each with butter and sprinkling with crumbs.
9. Mound apple mixture across the side of the phyllo nearest you (1-1/2 inches from the sides and ends).
10. Fold short ends of phyllo over filling.
11. Gently roll up the strudel tightly, enclosing the filling. Lift carefully onto prepared jelly-roll pan, seam side down.
12. Brush top with butter.
13. Bake for 35-40 minutes until golden brown.
14. Cool in pan on wire rack.
15. Use two spatulas to transfer to a cutting board.
16. To serve, sprinkle with confectioner's sugar and cut into 1-inch-thick slices.
